data.current_description:
An application can create users via the following call. You will need to replace clientid and clientsecret with the values you obtained when you registered and created your 1upHealth application (visit https://1up.health/dev/quick-start).

Each response will contain the new user's oneup_user_id, access_token, refresh_token, and app_user_id. The app_user_id helps you keep track of users between the 1up API and your own user management system. This must be a unique value for your application.
data.summary_suggestion: Create new user and obtain authentication tokens
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new user in your 1upHealth application. Requires client credentials (clientid and clientsecret) for authentication. Returns a new user object containing oneup_user_id (system-assigned), app_user_id (application-specific identifier), access_token, and refresh_token. The app_user_id must be unique within your application and is used to map users between 1upHealth and your system. Access tokens expire after 7200 seconds (2 hours).

data.current_summary: Generate New Authorization Code for Existing User
data.current_description:
If you need a new auth code for a user you already created on 1upHealth, you can make a request via the following method with clientid and clientsecret values replaced with your registered application's values via the environment variables in the upper right corner.

The code variable is the OAuth2 access code. You must exchange that to get the OAuth2 access token by following the the OAuth2 token grant steps. The access_token and refresh_token will be used to gain access to user data. Keep those secure via HIPAA compliant means of transmission and storage, along with all other patient data. The auth token expires after 7200 seconds (2 hours).
data.summary_suggestion: Generate new OAuth2 authorization code for existing user
data.description_suggestion: Generates a new OAuth2 authorization code for an existing user in your 1upHealth application. Requires client credentials (clientid and clientsecret) and the target user's identifier. Returns an authorization code that must be exchanged for access and refresh tokens via the OAuth2 token endpoint. The authorization code is single-use and short-lived. Resulting access tokens expire after 7200 seconds (2 hours). Tokens must be stored securely in HIPAA-compliant systems.
data.reasoning: Summary is too long and unclear. Description is verbose, mixes security guidance with functional details, and doesn't clearly specify required inputs or outputs. data.current_summary: Exchange Authorization Code for Access Token
data.current_description:
Use the following API call to exchange the OAuth Access Code received in "1. Create New User" or "2. Generate New Auth Code for Existing User".

The code variable is the OAuth2 access code. You must exchange that to get the OAuth2 access token by following the the OAuth2 token grant steps. The access_token and refresh_token will be used to gain access to user data. Keep those secure via HIPAA compliant means of transmission and storage, along with all other patient data. The auth token expires after 7200 seconds (2 hours).

You must also replace the values for clientid and clientsecret with values obtained when registering your application (visit https://1up.health/dev/quick-start).
data.summary_suggestion: Exchange authorization code for OAuth2 access token
data.description_suggestion: Exchanges an OAuth2 authorization code for access and refresh tokens. Requires the authorization code (obtained from user creation or authorization code generation), client credentials (clientid and clientsecret), and grant_type parameter. Returns access_token (valid for 7200 seconds), refresh_token (for obtaining new access tokens), and token_type. Access tokens must be stored securely and used in Authorization headers for subsequent API requests.
data.reasoning: Summary is acceptable but description is verbose and mixes security guidance with functional details. Enhanced version clarifies all required inputs (code, credentials, grant_type), specifies return values with their purposes and expiration, and explains how tokens are used.

data.current_description: An application can make a request to certain 1upHealth APIs with a bearer token which was created from executing the token endpoint above. This access token is safe to sit on the client side because its scope is limited to the user accessing it. However client secrets used to get the access token should remain on the server side. Available options are: dstu2, stu3, r4.
data.summary_suggestion: Make authenticated API request with OAuth2 bearer token
data.description_suggestion: Makes an authenticated request to 1upHealth APIs using an OAuth2 bearer token. Requires a valid access token in the Authorization header (format: "Bearer {access_token}"). Supports multiple FHIR versions via path parameter: dstu2, stu3, or r4. Returns FHIR resources accessible to the authenticated user. Access tokens are user-scoped and safe for client-side use; client secrets must remain server-side.

Once you have your OAuth Access Token, you can make the following call to obtain a bundle of available FHIR Resources (in this case Patient, but you can also obtain other resources as listed here - https://1up.health/dev/reference/fhir-resources). You can also include https://api.1up.health/fhir/dstu2/Patient/{{resource_id}} when you know the resource id.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve FHIR Patient resource bundle

Returns a bundle of Patient resources accessible to the authenticated user. Supports filtering by specific resource ID via path parameter. Supports multiple FHIR versions (dstu2, stu3, r4) via base path selection.

data.current_description:
Request the analytics bulk-data endpoint with the FHIR® $export operator to retrieve a list of bulk data files for your client application to download.

https://1up.health/dev/doc/fhir-bulk-data-export

This will return at least one bulk data download file for each resource type for which resources exist. This will only return resources that your given application client id and user id have access to. Optionally include a _type query parameter with a comma-separated list of FHIR® resources to limit the types of resources that you want to export, e.g.

https://analytics.1up.health/bulk-data/dstu2/$export?_type=Patient,Observation
data.summary_suggestion: Export bulk FHIR data for analytics
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a bulk data export operation to retrieve all FHIR resources accessible to the authenticated client application and user. Supports optional filtering via the _type query parameter to limit export to specific resource types (e.g., Patient, Observation). Returns a list of download URLs for bulk data files, with one file per resource type containing available data. The export respects the client application's and user's access permissions. This operation is useful for analytics, data migration, or batch processing workflows.

data.current_description:
The Provider Search API can be used to make custom provider search interface for patients or retrieve results returned by the above iframe. Plug your access_token (Check out our Quick Start Guide - https://1up.health/dev/quick-start - on how to get access tokens) generated with the client_id and client_secret for the applicaton in the header as a Bearer Token and search term as a query parameter in the url.

https://1up.health/dev/doc/provider-search-ui

Result is a list a health systems, clinics, hospitals or doctors for the searched term with 1uphealth system id which can be used to direct an user to a quick connect page or a patient portal (using the same access token) to initiate the login process. Check out our Quick Connect Documentation on how to quick connect.
data.summary_suggestion: Search for healthcare providers by name or location
data.description_suggestion: Searches for healthcare providers (doctors, clinics, hospitals, health systems) matching the provided search term. Requires OAuth Bearer token authentication. Accepts a search query parameter containing the provider name or location. Returns a list of matching providers with their 1upHealth system IDs, which can be used to initiate patient connections or direct users to provider portals. Results are filtered based on the authenticated application's access permissions.

An application can create users via the following call. You will need to replace clientid and clientsecret with the values you obtained when you registered and created your 1upHealth application (visit https://1up.health/dev/quick-start).

data.current_summary: Generate New Authorization Code for Existing User
data.current_description:
If you need a new auth code for a user you already created on 1upHealth, you can make a request via the following method with clientid and clientsecret values replaced with your registered application's values via the environment variables in the upper right corner.

The code variable is the OAuth2 access code. You must exchange that to get the OAuth2 access token by following the the OAuth2 token grant steps. The access_token and refresh_token will be used to gain access to user data. Keep those secure via HIPAA compliant means of transmission and storage, along with all other patient data. The auth token expires after 7200 seconds (2 hours).
data.summary_suggestion: Generate new OAuth2 authorization code for existing user
data.description_suggestion: Generates a new OAuth2 authorization code for an existing user in your 1upHealth application. Requires client credentials (clientid and clientsecret) and the target user's identifier. Returns an authorization code that must be exchanged for access and refresh tokens via the OAuth2 token endpoint. The authorization code is single-use and short-lived. Resulting access tokens expire after 7200 seconds (2 hours). Tokens must be stored securely in HIPAA-compliant systems.
data.reasoning: Summary is too long and unclear. Description is verbose, mixes security guidance with functional details, and doesn't clearly specify required inputs or outputs. data.current_summary: Exchange Authorization Code for Access Token
data.current_description:
Use the following API call to exchange the OAuth Access Code received in "1. Create New User" or "2. Generate New Auth Code for Existing User".

The code variable is the OAuth2 access code. You must exchange that to get the OAuth2 access token by following the the OAuth2 token grant steps. The access_token and refresh_token will be used to gain access to user data. Keep those secure via HIPAA compliant means of transmission and storage, along with all other patient data. The auth token expires after 7200 seconds (2 hours).

You must also replace the values for clientid and clientsecret with values obtained when registering your application (visit https://1up.health/dev/quick-start).
data.summary_suggestion: Exchange authorization code for OAuth2 access token
data.description_suggestion: Exchanges an OAuth2 authorization code for access and refresh tokens. Requires the authorization code (obtained from user creation or authorization code generation), client credentials (clientid and clientsecret), and grant_type parameter. Returns access_token (valid for 7200 seconds), refresh_token (for obtaining new access tokens), and token_type. Access tokens must be stored securely and used in Authorization headers for subsequent API requests.
data.reasoning: Summary is acceptable but description is verbose and mixes security guidance with functional details. Enhanced version clarifies all required inputs (code, credentials, grant_type), specifies return values with their purposes and expiration, and explains how tokens are used.

data.current_summary: API Request via OAuth2 Bearer Token Authentication
data.current_description: An application can make a request to certain 1upHealth APIs with a bearer token which was created from executing the token endpoint above. This access token is safe to sit on the client side because its scope is limited to the user accessing it. However client secrets used to get the access token should remain on the server side. Available options are: dstu2, stu3, r4.
data.summary_suggestion: Make authenticated API request with OAuth2 bearer token
data.description_suggestion: Makes an authenticated request to 1upHealth APIs using an OAuth2 bearer token. Requires a valid access token in the Authorization header (format: "Bearer {access_token}"). Supports multiple FHIR versions via path parameter: dstu2, stu3, or r4. Returns FHIR resources accessible to the authenticated user. Access tokens are user-scoped and safe for client-side use; client secrets must remain server-side.

data.current_description:
Request the analytics bulk-data endpoint with the FHIR® $export operator to retrieve a list of bulk data files for your client application to download.

https://1up.health/dev/doc/fhir-bulk-data-export

This will return at least one bulk data download file for each resource type for which resources exist. This will only return resources that your given application client id and user id have access to. Optionally include a _type query parameter with a comma-separated list of FHIR® resources to limit the types of resources that you want to export, e.g.

https://analytics.1up.health/bulk-data/dstu2/$export?_type=Patient,Observation

Returns a list of download URLs for bulk data files, with one file per resource type containing available data. The export respects the client application's and user's access permissions. This operation is useful for analytics, data migration, or batch processing workflows.
